pages.text: To make cheese, milk must be $(thing)curdled$() first. To curdle milk, you need $(thing)Rennet$(). Rennet comes from the stomach of $(thing)Ruminant$() animals. This includes $(l:the_world/wild_animals#yak)Yaks$(), $(l:the_world/wild_animals#cow)Cows$(), $(l:the_world/wild_animals#sheep)Sheep$(), $(l:the_world/wild_animals#goat)Goats$(), and $(l:the_world/wild_animals#musk_ox)Musk Oxen$(). To curdle milk, seal it in a $(item)Barrel$() with Rennet for 4 hours.

pages.title: Cheese Curds
pages.text: Curdled milk can then be converted to $(thing)Curds$() by sealing it in a barrel with $(thing)Cheesecloth$(). $(l)Cheesecloth is not reusable$().$(br)Cheese curds can be mixed with a few ingredients and fried to make $(item)Beer Battered Cheese Curds$() or aged into wet and dry cheeses.

pages.title: Cheese Wheels
pages.text: Cheese wheels are blocks made from $(item)Cheese Curds$(). To improve their quality and shelf life, cheese wheels should be $(thing)Aged$() in a $(l:tfc:cooking/decay#cellar)Cellar$(). In order to obtain edible cheese from a cheese wheel, it should be sliced off the wheel by clicking $(item)$(k:key.use)$() with a $(thing)Knife$(). If the block is broken, the aging is lost!$(br)$(thing)Dry Cheeses$() are made from curds and salt, while $(thing)Wet Cheeses$() are aged in sea water.
The aging stages of a wheel of Gouda: $(thing)Fresh$(), $(thing)Aged$(), and $(thing)Vintage$().
